overview.tagline: A small township with big accessibility and a business-friendly atmosphere
overview.description: The Township of South Hackensack is one of Bergen County's smallest municipalities, covering just over half a square mile along the Hackensack River. Despite its compact size, South Hackensack plays an outsized role as a commercial and industrial hub, home to the busy Route 46 corridor and numerous businesses. The township's small residential population enjoys easy highway access, low residential taxes relative to commercial ratables, and proximity to shopping and employment centers.
overview.schoolDistrict: South Hackensack Public Schools / Hackensack High School (sending district)
highlights: Strong commercial tax base helps keep residential taxes manageable
Strategic location along Route 46 with access to Route 17 and I-80
Proximity to Hackensack River and county parkland
Minutes from major employment and shopping centers

realEstate.assessmentInfo: Properties are assessed by the Bergen County Tax Assessor's office. South Hackensack benefits from a strong commercial ratable base that helps offset residential tax burdens.
realEstate.recentTrends: South Hackensack's residential real estate market is limited in inventory given the small size of the township. Homes that come to market tend to sell quickly due to the combination of affordable prices and excellent highway accessibility. The township has seen modest price appreciation in line with broader Bergen County trends.
neighborhoodNotes: Phillips Avenue area — Primary residential neighborhood with single-family homes
Green Street — Residential pocket near the Hackensack River
Route 46 corridor — Predominantly commercial with some mixed-use
Huyler Street area — Residential homes near the Teterboro border

trashCollection.title: Trash Collection
trashCollection.description: Curbside trash pickup is provided weekly to residential properties by the Township.
details: Weekly trash collection on designated days
Trash must be placed curbside by 6:00 AM on collection day
Use approved containers with secure lids
Bulk item pickup available by arrangement with DPW
Holiday schedule adjustments posted on township website

recycling.title: Recycling
recycling.description: Curbside recycling is collected on a regular schedule for all residential properties.
details: Commingled recyclables collected on designated days
Paper and cardboard recycled separately
Recycling containers provided by the township
Yard waste collection during growing season
Electronics recycling through Bergen County programs

waterSewer.title: Water & Sewer
waterSewer.description: Water and sewer services are provided to South Hackensack residents through regional utilities.
details: Water supplied by Veolia Water / Suez
Sewer service managed through the Bergen County Utilities Authority
Quarterly billing for water and sewer services
Water quality reports available from the water provider
Emergency service available 24/7

permits.title: Building & Permits
permits.description: The Building Department handles construction permits and code enforcement for the township.
details: Building permits required for construction and renovations
Zoning applications reviewed by the Planning Board
Commercial and industrial permits handled through the same office
Inspections scheduled through the Building Department
Certificate of Occupancy required for new construction

parking.title: Parking
parking.description: Parking in South Hackensack is primarily in commercial lots along Route 46 and on residential streets.
details: Street parking available in residential areas
Commercial parking lots along Route 46
Overnight parking restrictions on certain streets
Snow emergency parking bans enforced
No parking meters in the township

additionalServices.title: South Hackensack Free Public Library
additionalServices.description: Small community library serving residents with books, programs, and digital resources.
details: Located at 63 Phillips Avenue
Part of the Bergen County Cooperative Library System (BCCLS)
Children's programs and adult book clubs
Free Wi-Fi and computer access

noise.title: Noise Ordinance
noise.summary: South Hackensack enforces noise regulations to protect residential quality of life despite proximity to commercial corridors.
keyRules: Construction noise prohibited before 7:00 AM and after 7:00 PM on weekdays
Saturday construction allowed 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM only
No construction noise permitted on Sundays or federal holidays
Commercial operations must comply with noise limits at residential property lines
Loud music and amplified sound restricted after 10:00 PM in residential zones

propertyMaintenance.title: Property Maintenance
propertyMaintenance.summary: Property owners must maintain their properties in good condition.
keyRules: Properties must be maintained in a clean and orderly condition
Sidewalk snow removal required within 24 hours after snowfall
Fences must comply with zoning height limits
Abandoned vehicles must be removed from property
Commercial properties must maintain clean frontage

pets.title: Pet Regulations
pets.summary: South Hackensack requires dog licensing and enforces leash laws.
keyRules: All dogs over 7 months must be licensed annually with proof of rabies vaccination
Dogs must be leashed on all public property
Pet waste must be picked up immediately by the owner
No excessive barking that disturbs neighbors
Animal control services available through Bergen County

parking.title: Parking Regulations
parking.summary: Parking is regulated on township streets to ensure safety and access.
keyRules: Overnight parking restrictions on designated streets
Snow emergency parking bans — vehicles must be off-street
No commercial vehicles parked in residential areas overnight
No parking within 25 feet of intersections
Handicapped parking violations subject to fines

rentals.title: Rental Property Regulations
rentals.summary: Rental properties must comply with all applicable housing and safety codes.
keyRules: Rental units must meet housing code standards
Landlords responsible for property maintenance
Certificate of Occupancy required before rental occupancy
Fire safety inspections required for multi-family units
Short-term rentals subject to township regulations

other.title: Commercial Vehicle Restrictions
other.summary: Commercial and industrial vehicles are regulated in residential areas to preserve neighborhood character.
keyRules: Commercial vehicles over a certain weight prohibited from parking in residential zones
Truck routes designated on major roads only
No idling of commercial vehicles for extended periods in residential areas
Loading and unloading hours restricted near residential properties

schools.publicDistrict: South Hackensack Public Schools (K-8) / Hackensack High School (9-12, sending district)

publicSchools.name: Memorial School
publicSchools.notes: The only public school in South Hackensack serving PreK through 8th grade. Students attend Hackensack High School for grades 9-12.

whyMoveHere: Affordable housing prices relative to Bergen County
Strong commercial tax base helps offset residential property taxes
Excellent highway access via Route 46, Route 17, and I-80
Compact community with a small-town feel
Close proximity to employment centers and shopping

commuteToNYC.duration: 35-55 minutes to Port Authority Bus Terminal
commuteToNYC.notes: South Hackensack residents access NYC primarily via NJ Transit bus service along Route 46. The commute to the Port Authority Bus Terminal is approximately 35-55 minutes. Driving to the Secaucus Junction NJ Transit station is also a popular option for rail connections to Penn Station.

costOfLiving.summary: South Hackensack offers one of the more affordable options in Bergen County, with lower home prices and a commercial tax base that helps moderate residential property taxes.
costOfLiving.vsStateAverage: Approximately 10% above the New Jersey state average

pros: Very affordable by Bergen County standards
Strong commercial ratables keep residential taxes lower
Minutes from major shopping and services
cons: Very small township with limited amenities
No downtown shopping or dining district
Students attend Hackensack High School (sending district)

restaurants.name: Route 46 dining options
restaurants.description: The Route 46 corridor through South Hackensack features numerous chain and independent restaurants.

restaurants.name: South Hackensack Diner
restaurants.description: Classic diner serving breakfast, lunch, and dinner with traditional American fare.

restaurants.name: Sushi Palace
restaurants.description: Japanese restaurant offering sushi, sashimi, and hibachi in a casual setting along the Route 46 corridor.

parks.name: South Hackensack Municipal Park
parks.description: Township park with playground equipment, open green space, and a community gathering area.

parks.name: Hackensack River Greenway
parks.description: Portions of the Hackensack River Greenway trail system are accessible from South Hackensack, offering walking and nature viewing along the river.

parks.name: Bergen County Parks (nearby)
parks.description: Several Bergen County parks are within a short drive, including sections of the Hackensack River County Park.

events.name: Community Day
events.description: Annual township celebration bringing residents together with food, activities, and entertainment.

events.name: Holiday Celebration
events.description: Township holiday event with tree lighting and seasonal activities for families.

attractions.name: Hackensack River
attractions.description: The Hackensack River runs along the eastern border of the township, providing scenic views and opportunities for kayaking and fishing.

attractions.name: Route 46 Shopping Corridor
attractions.description: The commercial corridor along Route 46 offers a wide variety of retail stores, restaurants, and services.

shoppingAreas: Route 46 corridor — Major commercial strip with retail and dining
Hackensack downtown — Shopping and restaurants nearby
Garden State Plaza (Paramus) — Major mall a short drive north

government.meetingSchedule: Township Committee meets the 2nd and 4th Tuesday of each month at 7:00 PM
government.meetingLocation: Township Hall, 227 Phillips Avenue, South Hackensack, NJ 07606

importantNumbers.label: Bergen County Office of Emergency Management
